target.name: Nuvacore
target.founder: Gerard Williams III, John Bruno, Ram Srinivasan

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nuvacore is a seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Gerard Williams III, John Bruno, and Ram Srinivasan. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparable companies across the Chips & Semiconductors sector: three public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and eight private peers including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The target company's narrow peer set—same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three comparable datapoints despite 10 same-sector peers existing. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the wide dispersion in scale, technical focus, and maturity across the broader semiconductor landscape. Without sufficient narrow comparables, traditional multiples-based triangulation cannot be reliably anchored.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range given the constraints. The primary uncertainty is the absence of adequate seed-stage or similarly-scaled precedents within the semiconductor peer universe to establish a credible quantitative benchmark. Any valuation conclusion would require either: (i) expanding the dataset to identify overlooked comparables at matching stage and scale, or (ii) applying alternative methods (e.g., discounted cash flow, if financial projections are available) that do not depend on peer-relative anchoring. Further data collection is necessary before a defensible range can be established.
